text:
Rain could not dampen the spirits at the opening ceremony of the Paris Olympic Games, with 1.5 billion viewers tuning in to watch tradition being broken as some 10,500 athletes from 184 countries were paraded along the River Seine to performances from Lady Gaga, Celine Dion and French talent.

France's bold move to hold the Olympic Opening Ceremony outside for the first time has been hailed a success, with athletes and spectators around the world treated to one of the most colourful and spectacular starts to the 17-day event.

Hundreds of thousands of French locals, tourists and global dignitaries braved the wet weather to line the quays and banks of the River Seine in the capital on Friday night (local time) to officially kickstart the 2024 Paris Games under overcast conditions.

The four-hour ceremony was broken up into 12 sections in what organisers described as a storybook to showcase France's rich history, culture, gastronomy and spirit.

Proceedings opened to a colourful start, with the hues of France's flag exploding from Pont d'Austerlitz to begin the parade of some 10,500 athletes from 184 nations.

A boat carrying Greece's representative emerged first - a tradition of the opening ceremony because that is where the Olympics was born - on the River Seine.

It was followed by the remaining countries in alphabetical order, according to the host country's language which in this case is French.

However, Australian viewers had to wait till almost the end of the parade to see the packed vessel of home-grown athletes dressed in green and gold.

The reason is because officials want to put an emphasis on the two future host countries of the Summer Games - the United States and Australia - to the audience.

Around 80 Australian athletes were packed on the boat, with flagbearers Jessica Fox and Eddie Ockenden proudly waving the Aussie flag and cheering loudly.

Closely followed behind was the largest team in the Games and the 2028 hosts, the US, with its competitors decked out in custom-made Ralph Lauren outfits.

And last to emerge were the hosts France.

Spectators were treated to fun, colourful and lively performances, such as the can-can, musical acts and even a fashion show highlighting France's culture.

Lady Gaga then put on a show-stopping performance singing the French classic Mon truc en plumes by Zizi Jeanmaire on a gold staircase near the Eiffel Tower.

The Poker Face singer wore a black strapless gown with matching gloves and was flanked by male and female dancers carrying pink pom poms and feathers.

Meanwhile, viewers at home were treated to a history lesson of France while a mysterious figure dressed in a black and white ensemble carried the Olympic torch.

Pre-filmed scenes showed the character run and perform parkour moves along rooves, including the famous Notre Dame which was damaged by fire in 2019.

After the six-kilometre parade along the Seine, athletes filed into grandstand-style seating next to the Eiffel Tower for the rest of the proceedings.

A Gendarmerie officer rode along the river on a silver metal horse carrying the Olympic Games flag to be hoisted up a flagpole.

After speeches from the Olympic boss, French President Emmanuel Macron officially opened the games: “I declare the Games of the XXXIII Olympiad open!”

The mysterious character from earlier then handed the torch to legendary French footballer Zinedine Zidane.

The 52-year-old soccer coach the passed the torch onto Spanish tennis icon Rafael Nadal, as he prepares for his final Games in his decorated 23-year career.

Olympics: ðŸ”¥ The final torchbearers are advancing towards the Paris 2024 Olympic cauldronâ€¦@RafaelNadal@serenawilliams@Carl_Lewis@nadiacomaneci10If youâ€™re not already tuning in, watch the lighting moment live right here:ðŸ”—https://t.co/M6VR8BPFVnâ€¦pic.twitter.com/n7p6dlLaNy

It was immediately followed by the illumination of the Eiffel Tower, with a spectacular laser show lighting up Paris' night sky.

Nadal carried the torch onto a speed boat with fellow tennis legend Serena Williams, US track and field great Carl Lewis and Romanian gymnast Nadia Comaneci on board.

The flame was then moved to the ground, with several French Olympians taking turns to carry the torch through the famous streets of Paris to the cauldron which is aligned with the Louvre, Place de la Concorde, the Champs-Élysées, and the Arc de Triomphe.

It was then lit up by French judo champion Teddy Riner and retired track and field sprinter Marie-José Pérec, as a hot air balloon raised it some 30 metres high.

ðŸ¤©@celinedionis spectacular and Paris sparkles with the Olympic spirit! âœ¨Paris 2024 is here!!! ðŸŽ‰#Paris2024#OpeningCeremonypic.twitter.com/es3KmnGD2k

The Opening Ceremony capped off with a goosebump-raising performance from Celin Dion, 56, who was perched on top of the Eiffel Tower.

The Canadian singer - who was diagnosed with stiff person syndrome which can easily lead to injuries from falls - sung Hymne à l’amour (the Hymn to Love) by Edith Piaf.

The four-hour event is rumoured to have cost (AUD)$2.5 billion, but appears to have paid off for the French with fans and commentators hailing it a success.

All eyes will now turn to the first Olympic events on Sunday.

title: World's athletes take it all in as they are paraded along Paris' Seine River for never-before-seen Olympic Games Opening Ceremony | Sky News Australia
description: France's bold move to hold the Olympic Opening Ceremony outside for the first time has been hailed a success, with athletes and spectators around the world treated to one of the most colourful and spectacular starts to the 17-day event.
